timeline.label: NAV-RELPOSNED stream starts with carr_soln=none, rel_pos_valid=0, rel_pos_heading_valid=0 — dual-antenna moving-baseline RTK NOT resolved from the first sample
Diagnostic ERROR ekf_se_map: odometry/filtered topic status = 'No events recorded' — the localization EKF never publishes an output for the entire session
Diagnostic WARN ublox_rover: TMODE3 'Not configured' and ublox_moving_base: TMODE3 'Not configured' — moving-baseline time-mode never set up
Diagnostic ERROR ublox_moving_base: fix topic 'Frequency too high; Timestamps too far in past seen' — moving-base sensor stream already unhealthy at t~4s
/ntrip_client first burst of RTCM checksum mismatches (expected/actual mismatches) — correction stream begins corrupting
Diagnostic ERROR ublox_moving_base: fix topic 'Frequency too low; Timestamps too far in past seen'
/ntrip_client 'Unable to send NMEA sentence to server'; Exception: timed out — NTRIP caster link degraded
/ntrip_client restart: 'NMEA sentence failed to send to server 5 times, restarting'; 'RTCM requested before client was connected, returning empty list'
/ntrip_client ERROR (level=8) 'RTCM data not received for 4 seconds, reconnecting'

Session ends. Across entire 3626.8 s run: throttle.enabled=0 for 36208/36208 samples, brake.enabled=0 for 36210/36210 — drive-by-wire autonomy never engaged; carr_soln='none' for 18133/18133 NAV-RELPOSNED; rel_pos_heading_valid=0 for 18133/18133; diff_soln=1 only 15.0% of time; rover num_sv stays in 19–32 and h_acc in 0.47–1.30 m throughout (no tunnel-like sky-blockage signature). No tunnel is visible in any sampled camera frame (0000s, 1376s, 2501s, 3502s show urban street, highway, parking lot).

hypotheses.summary: Dual-antenna moving-baseline RTK is mis-configured: carr_soln never leaves 'none' and rel_pos_heading_valid is 0 for every one of 18,133 NAV-RELPOSNED messages, so RTK heading is unavailable from t=0 — a session-wide baseline/configuration defect, not a tunnel event.

evidence.snippet: carr_soln=none, rel_pos_valid=0, rel_pos_heading_valid=0 at t=0.243 s and identical for all 18133 rows
WARN 'ublox_rover: TMODE3 Not configured' and 'ublox_moving_base: TMODE3 Not configured' at t=0.52 s
fix_type=3 (3D) and num_sv 19–32 throughout — sky visibility is fine, RTK lock failure is not a sats/tunnel issue
Open sky / urban / parking scenes; no tunnel in any of the 29 sampled thumbnails

hypotheses.patch_hint: Provision TMODE3 + moving-baseline on both F9Ps (rover receives UBX-RXM-RTCM from moving-base over UART2) and force MSM7/1230 RTCM output before the run is considered autonomy-ready.

hypotheses.summary: NTRIP correction stream is chronically unhealthy (~100 RTCM checksum mismatches, a 20 s server-restart at t≈1664 s, and a 'RTCM not received for 4 s, reconnecting' ERROR at t≈3348 s) so diff_soln=1 only 15.0 % of the session; even the DGPS augmentation is intermittent.

evidence.snippet: /ntrip_client 'Unable to send NMEA sentence to server' + 'Exception: timed out'
/ntrip_client level=8 ERROR 'RTCM data not received for 4 seconds, reconnecting'
diff_soln=1 in only 2715/18133 samples (15.0%); carr_soln never advances past 'none'

hypotheses.patch_hint: Harden ntrip_client: back-off-and-retry on checksum failure instead of discarding packets silently, add a watchdog that raises a diagnostic ERROR after N consecutive bad frames, and validate that the mountpoint delivers the correction types needed for carrier-phase (1077/1087/1230).

hypotheses.summary: /vehicle/gps/fix (Lincoln MKZ on-board GPS bridge) publishes a frozen latitude of −3e-7° for all 3591 messages while longitude/altitude vary normally — a stuck/zero-initialized lat field that downstream nodes consume without validation.

evidence.snippet: t=0.400 s lat=-0.0000003 lon=-58.5305920 alt=-42.500 ; identical lat across all 3591 rows, lon has 2557 distinct values
True latitude is ≈-34.4453° (Buenos Aires, San Isidro) — /vehicle/gps/fix is publishing 0° lat, not the real value

hypotheses.patch_hint: In the NavSatFix bridge reject messages with |lat|<1e-3° or status<STATUS_FIX instead of forwarding them; add a covariance-based sanity gate in the EKF input.

hypotheses.summary: Drive-by-wire enable bit is 0 for every throttle and brake report (36208 and 36210 samples) — the autonomy stack never took control, so the operator's perceived 'behavior degradation' cannot be attributed to autonomous behavior at all; a DBW handshake / EKF-availability gate likely blocked engage.

evidence.snippet: enabled=0 in 36208/36208 rows; single transition at t=0.194 s to 0 and never to 1
ekf_se_map: odometry/filtered 'No events recorded' — localization pre-req for engage never satisfied

hypotheses.patch_hint: Emit a loud, structured 'engage blocked because <reason>' diagnostic when the engage pre-req chain (EKF alive && RTK heading valid && DBW ready) fails, so operators don't mis-attribute the outage to an unrelated environmental cause.

hypotheses.summary: Operator-proposed 'GPS anomaly at tunnel entry' hypothesis — REFUTED: no tunnel appears in any of the 29 sampled frames, num_sv stays healthy (≥19) for the full hour, and every RTK/localization fault is observed from t<1 s, not at a localized tunnel-entry moment.

evidence.snippet: Parking lot, open highway, urban avenue with 'Iglesia Cristiana de San Isidro' storefront, parking lot — all open-sky; no tunnel
num_sv bucket medians 26–31 over 300 s windows for full session; no sky-blockage drop
RTK failure (carr_soln=none) present at first sample t=0.243 s — predates any possible tunnel entry

hypotheses.patch_hint: Update the incident triage runbook: before accepting a localized-event narrative, require that the symptom first appear after the nominal good-state window.

patch_proposal:
--- a/launch/ublox_moving_baseline.yaml
+++ b/launch/ublox_moving_baseline.yaml
@@ ublox_moving_base:
   device: /dev/ttyUSB_mb
   frame_id: gps_mb
-  # tmode3: 0   # disabled (was default)
+  tmode3: 0         # 0=Disabled is correct, but MUST be explicitly set so diagnostics stop emitting 'TMODE3 Not configured'
   uart2:
     baudrate: 460800
-    in_protocol:  { ubx: true, rtcm3x: true, nmea: false }
-    out_protocol: { ubx: true, rtcm3x: false, nmea: false }
+    in_protocol:  { ubx: true, rtcm3x: true, nmea: false }
+    out_protocol: { ubx: true, rtcm3x: true, nmea: false }   # moving-base MUST emit RTCM3 4072.0/4072.1 on UART2 for the rover
   publish:
-    nav: { pvt: true, status: true }
+    nav: { pvt: true, status: true, relposned: false }
+    rxm: { rtcm: true }                                      # expose UBX-RXM-RTCM so we can diagnose dropped corrections
   rate: 5   # Hz — must match diagnostics expected_rate (was 8Hz, hence 'Frequency too high')

--- a/launch/ublox_rover.yaml
+++ b/launch/ublox_rover.yaml
@@ ublox_rover:
   device: /dev/ttyUSB_rover
   frame_id: gps_rover
-  # tmode3: 0
+  tmode3: 0
   uart2:
     baudrate: 460800
-    in_protocol:  { ubx: true, rtcm3x: false, nmea: false }
+    in_protocol:  { ubx: true, rtcm3x: true, nmea: false }   # rover must accept RTCM 4072.x from moving-base for moving-baseline carrier solution
   publish:
     nav: { pvt: true, status: true, relposned: true, heading: true }
     rxm: { rtcm: true }
   rate: 5

--- a/ntrip_client/ntrip_client.py
+++ b/ntrip_client/ntrip_client.py
@@ class NtripClient:
     def _read_packet(self):
         pkt = self._sock.recv(BUFSIZE)
-        if crc24q(pkt[:-3]) != pkt[-3:]:
-            rospy.logwarn('Expected Checksum: 0x%X', expected)
-            rospy.logwarn('Actual Checksum:   0x%X', actual)
-            return None  # silently drop
+        if crc24q(pkt[:-3]) != pkt[-3:]:
+            self._bad_crc_streak += 1
+            rospy.logwarn_throttle(5.0, 'RTCM CRC mismatch (%d in a row)', self._bad_crc_streak)
+            if self._bad_crc_streak >= 10:
+                self._diag.broadcast(DiagnosticStatus.ERROR,
+                                     'NTRIP correction stream corrupt, RTK will not fix')
+                self._reconnect()
+            return None
+        self._bad_crc_streak = 0
         return pkt

--- a/localization/engage_gate.py
+++ b/localization/engage_gate.py
@@ def can_engage(self):
-    return self.dbw_ready and self.ekf_ready
+    reasons = []
+    if not self.dbw_ready:      reasons.append('dbw_not_ready')
+    if not self.ekf_ready:      reasons.append('ekf_no_odom')
+    if not self.rtk_heading_ok: reasons.append('rtk_heading_invalid (carr_soln=%s, rel_pos_heading_valid=%s)' % (self.carr_soln, self.rel_pos_heading_valid))
+    if reasons:
+        self._diag.broadcast(DiagnosticStatus.ERROR, 'engage blocked: ' + ','.join(reasons))
+        return False
+    return True

--- a/drivers/mkz_gps_bridge.cpp
+++ b/drivers/mkz_gps_bridge.cpp
@@ void publish_fix(const RawGps& g) {
-  msg.latitude  = g.lat;
-  msg.longitude = g.lon;
-  msg.altitude  = g.alt;
-  pub_.publish(msg);
+  if (std::abs(g.lat) < 1e-3 || g.status < sensor_msgs::NavSatStatus::STATUS_FIX) {
+    ROS_WARN_THROTTLE(5.0, "Dropping /vehicle/gps/fix: lat=%.7f status=%d (stuck-zero guard)", g.lat, g.status);
+    return;
+  }
+  msg.latitude  = g.lat;
+  msg.longitude = g.lon;
+  msg.altitude  = g.alt;
+  pub_.publish(msg);
 }
